target.name: release-gate
target.source_summary: templates/release-gate.md + skills/testing/check-release-gate.md (Test Hierarchy, Critical Areas, Rules) + agents/tester.md + CLAUDE.md rule 6

gates.description: Test tiers ran in order — smoke before API before E2E — with no tier skipped for the event type. Each gate blocks the next; an out-of-order or skipped tier is binary.
Decision PASS while the blocking-issues list is non-empty, or while any critical area's evidence is marked missing. Incoherent by definition — 'known blockers must be fixed, not narrated away.'

Every critical area (auth/authz, payments/billing, state integrity, data safety, deployment correctness, error-response quality) is marked verified-with-cited-evidence, missing (and therefore blocking), or not-applicable-with-reason. Missing evidence treated as passing fails this gate.
Exactly one decision, PASS or FAIL, stated.

dimensions.description: Every claim in the gate maps to a specific, retrievable piece of evidence.

anchors.1: Evidence section narrates: 'tests passed', 'manually checked' — nothing retrievable.
anchors.3: Names suites and artifacts, but at least one critical claim has no linked run/log/audit.
anchors.5: Every claim maps to a specific test run, audit finding, or log entry that exists in the evidence record.

dimensions.description: Previously known blockers are closed by passing evidence, with regression coverage.

anchors.1: Blockers described as resolved with no re-test ('narrated away').
anchors.3: Re-tested and passing, but no test-for-the-bug was added (write tests for bugs before fixing them).
anchors.5: Each fixed blocker has a regression test that exists and passes, cited.

dimensions.description: Blocking and non-blocking issues are correctly classified.

anchors.1: Cosmetic items listed as blockers or blockers listed as acceptable — classification noise in both directions.
anchors.5: Classification matches the critical-areas standard exactly; cosmetic items tracked, visible, and explicitly non-blocking.

dimensions.description: The decision is justified against the evidence balance.

anchors.3: Summary restates counts ('12 passed, 0 failed') without tying to critical areas.
anchors.5: One-two sentences connecting the decision to the specific evidence and any accepted residual risk.

aggregation.gate_handling: Apply gate caps after normalization; lowest applicable cap wins.

weight_rationale: evidence_traceability is heaviest because the release gate exists solely to convert evidence into a ship decision (rule 6: 'deployment follows passing evidence, not optimism') — a gate whose claims cannot be traced is optimism with a template.

unmeasurable_rules.rule: Never skip tests to deploy faster (behavior)
unmeasurable_rules.home: trajectory/tester.rubric.json :: harness_run_before_findings gate + tier_order deterministic gate here

unmeasurable_rules.rule: Fail closed on unproven critical behavior (behavior)

known_good.summary: FAIL decision with traced evidence and one honest gap.
known_good.content: Decision: FAIL. Tiers: smoke passed (run sm-118, 0:42), API passed (run api-119), E2E passed (run e2e-120). Critical areas: auth verified (api-119 cases 401/403); error responses verified (429 includes usage/limit/reset/upgrade — api-119#14); state integrity MISSING — concurrent claim test not yet written; billing N/A (no billing surface in scope). Blockers: 1) state-integrity evidence missing for workers/export.js claim path. Non-blocking: log prefix inconsistency. Recommendation: fix blockers first — write concurrent-claim regression test, then re-gate.

known_bad.summary: PASS on confidence with a skipped tier and an open blocker.
known_bad.content: Decision: PASS. Evidence: smoke passed; skipped API tests to save time since E2E covers it; E2E mostly passed. Auth: should be fine, it was working yesterday. Blockers: session fixation issue from last review (engineer says it's fixed). Recommendation: ship now.
